title: skill-atoms v1 skill atom
description: A skill atom is a discoverable, invocable unit of AI capability. It bundles a bounded task description, a system-prompt fragment the agent loads on invocation, and metadata for catalog routing, versioning, and trust gating.

type.description: skill — a single bounded capability; skill-set — a named group of related skills loaded together; invocation-contract — a schema describing how a skill may be called (inputs, outputs, side-effects).

id.description: Stable, human-readable identifier. Use kebab-case segments separated by forward slashes, e.g. 'code/code-review'.

version.description: SemVer. Patch for prompt tweaks; minor for new fields; major for breaking signature changes.

description.description: What this skill does and when to use it.

system_prompt_fragment.description: The instruction text injected into the agent's system prompt when this skill is loaded. Write in second person imperative. Must be self-contained — do not reference other atoms by assumption.

applicable_domains.description: Routing tags used by the Olympus broker to select skills. Examples: code, review, debug, write, commit, deploy.

invocation_contract.description: Optional formal contract describing inputs, outputs, and side-effects for programmatic invocation.

authored_by.description: Signing key identifier of the atom's primary author.

superseded_by.description: ID of the skill that replaces this one when lifecycle is deprecated or retired.

depends_on.description: Slugs of skills this skill depends on. Installing this skill also installs all listed dependencies.

side_effects.description: Human-readable descriptions of observable side effects (e.g. 'writes a file', 'calls an external API').
